name: Portobello mushrooms with brioche and poached eggs
intro: then get the eggs poaching. This works as well as a starter late in the day as it

ingredients.item: ground cinnamon flaked sea salt and black pepper
ground red pepper, plus extra to serve
plus 2 tbsp/100g sour cream, to serve

method: Mix the mushrooms with 3 tbsp of the oil, 1 garlic clove, ¬º tsp of the cinnamon, ¬Ω tsp ÔÄÇ aked salt, and a good grind of pepper. Spread out on a large parchment-lined baking sheet and roast for 15 minutes, stirring after 7 minutes, until soft and starting to brown. Toss with the basil and set aside.

While the mushrooms are in the oven, mix the remaining 2 tbsp of oil with the remaining ¬º tsp of cinnamon, 1 garlic clove, the pepper ÔÄÇ akes, and ¬º tsp of ÔÄÇ aked salt. Brush the oil and spices on one side of the brioche slices and place on a separate parchment-lined baking sheet, brushed side up. With about 6 or 7 minutes left for the mushrooms, put the bread into the oven alongside the mushrooms and toast until the bread is golden brown and crisp.

Meanwhile, ÔÄÅ ll a medium saucepan with plenty of water and bring to a boil over high heat. Once boiling, decrease the heat to medium-high and carefully break in the eggs. Poach for 1¬Ω minutes for a runny yolk (or a little longer for a ÔÄÅ rmer set).

Divide the brioche among four plates and top each slice with the mushrooms so that they are all ready. Using a slotted spoon, remove the eggs from the water and spoon them on top of the mushrooms. Sprinkle each egg with a pinch of salt and a pinch of pepper ÔÄÇ akes and serve warm, with a spoonful of sour cream alongside.

This was brought onto our menu as a vegan option for breakfast. It‚Äôs been ÔÄÇying off the pass to all of our customers, vegan or not, ever since, as an alternative to eggs. We serve it on thick slices of grilled sourdough bread with a fresh green salad alongside. It‚Äôs also lovely with a sprinkle of crispy fried shallots. If you think you‚Äôll get into the habit of making this for breakfast, double or quadruple the harissa onions‚Äîa batch keeps well in the fridge for around 5 days and turns this into a meal that can be ready in 5 minutes. Thanks to Claire Hodgson.
